primary_source.title: Event Timing API
primary_source.source_limits: Work in progress; browser support and implementation behavior must be verified independently.

finding: PerformanceEventTiming can group event entries by non-zero interactionId and decompose latency into input delay, processing duration, and presentation delay. An interactionId is correlation within a page lifecycle, not a stable global identifier or proof of causal source code.

prior_work_comparison.new_scope: interaction-level grouping and separate latency-component verdicts
prior_work_comparison.non_duplication: The new artifact does not replace long-animation-frame evidence; it supplies the missing user-interaction identity and latency decomposition layer.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit source and tests
cases: related events are grouped and bounded interaction accepted
duration, input, processing, and presentation violations remain distinct

trust_limits: Passing applies only to retained entries for the exact page lifecycle and tested sequence.
Buffered Event Timing entries default to a higher duration threshold; the observer requests 16ms for future entries.
No grouped interaction is inconclusive rather than passing.
Event Timing does not independently prove perceived harm or identify definitive source-code causality.
falsification_route: Run the exact gesture on the target device, retain buffered and live PerformanceEventTiming entries, and reject acceptance if instrumentation is unsupported, no non-zero interactionId is observed, or any declared latency component budget is exceeded.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health data, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploaded content included.
